110631.explanation: Consultation is important, but through participation and village discussions. Referenda only for exceptional topics.

110634.explanation: Tailored approach per location. Not everything fits everywhere.

110637.explanation: We await national policy. Shelter requires a tailored approach. The form, size, and location must suit Bladel and be carefully coordinated with residents and partners.

110638.explanation: Housing does not have to be located on the company's own premises. Suitable alternatives such as migrant hotels or other qualitative facilities are often better integrated and cause less nuisance in the area.

110639.explanation: Compensation is logical, with attention to quality and location.

110640.explanation: Allowed, but with attention to health and nuisance.

110642.explanation: A municipal fireworks show is not a core task of the municipality. Initiatives can come from residents or associations, but the municipality does not have to organize this itself.

110646.explanation: Only through clear frameworks and spatial considerations.

110650.explanation: Heritage is part of identity and livability.

110651.explanation: Very dependent on use. Coordinate well with residents what is desirable and feasible.

110652.explanation: Use targeted where needed, no automatic expansion.

110653.explanation: Only in places where it demonstrably helps.

110657.explanation: This does not structurally solve the housing shortage.

110659.explanation: Encourage participation, but with tailored solutions.

110660.explanation: Support remains necessary for low incomes.

110663.explanation: Gradually and where there is demand.

110664.explanation: Consider per street, safety comes first.

110665.explanation: Care must be aligned with the individual.

110667.explanation: Only if it really cannot be helped otherwise.

110677.explanation: Does not have to be rental. Social purchase is also allowed.

110678.explanation: Does not fit the village character.

110679.explanation: Address where there is nuisance, no generic ban.

110683.explanation: But also optimally use existing sites.

110684.explanation: Only if this is technically, financially, and traffic-wise feasible.

110631.explanation: The topics we discuss in the municipality are often not simply for or against. That is why we find a referendum inappropriate. It is very important, however, that residents are heard before a decision is made.

110634.explanation: The entrepreneur can perfectly well decide for themselves whether this suits their company.

110637.explanation: We are certainly against this. Only if the dispersion law remains in effect for a long time will we as the municipality of Bladel have to comply. Then we prefer dispersion across our villages rather than one (large) asylum seekers' center.

110638.explanation: This does not have to be on the employer's own premises, but in our view employers share responsibility for decent housing of labor migrants.

110639.explanation: This does not have to be one-to-one with us. For example, we already see new nature emerging around the meandering streams in our municipality.

110640.explanation: A municipality should not prohibit this, but it is important that residents take each other into account.

110642.explanation: A very good idea now that there is a fireworks ban for the coming New Year's Eve. We would also like to see space given to an association or foundation to organize a fireworks show for the whole village on New Year's Eve.

110645.explanation: This is important, but in our view the financial responsibility lies with the entrepreneur.

110646.explanation: We need to build much more, but we think it is good that the municipality keeps control over where building is allowed.

110650.explanation: It is important that we honor our history. We do this partly by properly maintaining our monuments.

110651.explanation: It is important that we have a library in the municipality. But we leave it to the library itself to decide whether this is through one or two branches.

110652.explanation: We currently have enough enforcement officers. We do not want to become a municipality where people are constantly checked.

110653.explanation: This does not fit with our village character. We are open to installing cameras at very specific locations to prevent nuisance.

110657.explanation: Holiday homes should not be used as permanent residences. But given the current housing shortage, we think tolerating this is appropriate.

110659.explanation: It seems logical to us that people receiving social assistance contribute to society through volunteer work. This can also be a step towards paid employment.

110663.explanation: These are already rarely occupied. For specific applications, the municipality should take this into consideration.

110664.explanation: This is already the case in many places; only the ring road in Bladel may have a higher speed limit. We want to keep it that way.

110665.explanation: This does not fit with our country. You are simply helped by the doctor on duty.

110667.explanation: We want the property tax (ozb) in the municipality of Bladel to remain well below the national average.

110669.explanation: This is already rising towards the national average in the coming years but need not increase further from our perspective.

110675.explanation: Fine if a school pays attention to this, but the municipality does not need to actively contribute.

110676.explanation: We should make it harder, not easier, for our youth to come into contact with (soft) drugs.

110677.explanation: We prefer to build affordable owner-occupied homes. That suits a municipality like Bladel better.

110678.explanation: This does not fit the village character of our municipality. We may build somewhat higher in the centers, as we have done at Markstaete.

110679.explanation: This occurs rarely in our municipality.

110682.explanation: We must be able to get there. If it is purely about Sniederslaan, it could become car-restricted.

110683.explanation: This does not have to be located in Bladel, but we must tackle this across the Kempen region. We must do this for our locally rooted entrepreneurs.

110684.explanation: This could be a nice solution, but the disadvantage is that you then cannot turn anymore. Besides, we have just completed the entire process of improving the flow on the N284.

110686.explanation: We must make building a care home for informal caregivers much easier. Within the built-up area, this is already fairly easy in terms of regulations; we also want it to be facilitated more easily in the rural area.

110631.explanation: Involving residents is essential. The VHP chooses good environmental dialogues and strong citizen participation over binding referenda, recognizing that there is still much to improve in participation.

110634.explanation: The VHP is positive about extra activities at farms, such as a shop or campsite, provided they fit within the environmental vision. Operating in the countryside is becoming increasingly difficult; therefore, we should not unnecessarily hinder farmers. Responsible diversification is encouraged.

110637.explanation: The VHP takes responsibility but follows national policy. Small scale, integration, and safety are conditions.

110638.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for housing seasonal workers, but this does not have to be on their own company grounds. These employees deserve quality and humane accommodation.

110639.explanation: Greenery is very important in our rural environment, and therefore the VHP attaches great value to the green standard. At the same time, a project should not be unnecessarily hindered by a rigid replanting obligation; customization remains necessary.

110640.explanation: At this time, residents are looking for different ways to heat their homes; this should not be simply prohibited. As long as safety is guaranteed and nuisance is limited, wood burning should remain possible.

110642.explanation: Community money must be spent carefully and should not just be wasted.

110645.explanation: Accessibility for people with disabilities is important, but the responsibility lies with entrepreneurs. The municipality can advise but should not pay.

110646.explanation: The VHP is neither fundamentally for nor against this. Farmers should be able to build homes on their land under conditions, provided this fits within the environmental vision or via careful customization, for example with a BOPA.

110650.explanation: Historically, there has been limited investment here; more effort is needed rather than less. Monuments are an important part of our shared cultural history.

110651.explanation: The VHP supports good facilities in all villages. It is not feasible to have a library in every village, but closing a library is undesirable.

110652.explanation: It is important that residents feel safe. More enforcement officers increase visibility and make it possible to take on other tasks that are currently often neglected.

110653.explanation: Camera surveillance is especially suitable for large cities. In Bladel, it is more important that residents look out for each other and do not feel constantly monitored, but take joint responsibility for safety.

110657.explanation: In the current housing crisis, sometimes creative solutions are needed. Provided they are well regulated and have fair rules compared to other homeowners, this can make a positive contribution.

110659.explanation: It is important to involve as many residents as possible, even if that cannot be through the labor market but socially. Where possible, it is therefore desirable that people receiving benefits contribute to society.

110660.explanation: Residents with a low income also deserve a place in our community and often contribute a lot. Where appropriate, the municipality should support these residents.

110663.explanation: If residents choose an electric car, we must support them in this. Due to grid congestion, unnecessary public charging points are undesirable; charging on private property is preferred.

110664.explanation: It is important that children can play outside safely, pedestrians can cross safely, and cyclists feel safe in traffic.

110665.explanation: Residents are free to choose their healthcare provider. However, as a small municipality, we cannot offer all forms of care, so some residents will have to travel further.

110667.explanation: Due to national policy, more is expected from municipalities with fewer resources. Since costs rise mostly with inflation, it is logical that property tax (OZB) is also adjusted to follow inflation.

110669.explanation: The tourist tax should be brought in line with the national average; Bladel is currently below this. A gradual increase towards that level is supported by the VHP.

110675.explanation: Children do not have to speak the dialect, but attention to awareness and knowledge of our shared cultural history is valuable.

110676.explanation: As long as this can be organized in a socially safe way and does not cause nuisance, the VHP is not opposed to it.

110677.explanation: Besides being a national obligation, our municipality is lagging behind in social housing. The demand is high, and therefore we must focus heavily on this.

110678.explanation: Higher buildings should be possible in suitable locations, but truly tall apartment blocks do not fit the rural character of our municipality.

110679.explanation: It is up to tenants themselves whether they want to rent a room. For some target groups this is desirable and therefore the municipality should not prohibit it.

110682.explanation: A certain number of cars must remain possible. The VHP therefore prefers a car-reduced solution, where surrounding traffic flow is significantly improved.

110683.explanation: Bladel and the Kempen play an important role in the regional manufacturing industry. To continue offering local space for both SMEs and larger companies to locate and expand, a second Kempisch Business Park is necessary.

110684.explanation: The VHP prefers a tunnel at the N284 near De Lange Trekken. This also relieves the intersection at Van Mossel and involves fewer complications from cables and pipes underground.

110686.explanation: We should not make building a caregiving dwelling unnecessarily difficult. A good environmental dialogue is important; permits can provide the needed transparency and involvement of the surroundings.

110631.explanation: A referendum with only the choice 'for or against' provides too little insight into what residents find important. Decisions must be made with broad support and a careful consideration of interests. The municipal council is elected for this and represents all residents.

110634.explanation: We definitely want to support farmers who want to partly or fully switch to a different way of working and business activities. New business activities must fit the environment.

110637.explanation: We believe we must be solidary and adhere to the distribution law that still applies. We also find it important that there is sufficient and appropriate support at a reception center, so residents can start as well as possible.

110638.explanation: Good housing should certainly be arranged by employers. If there are possibilities on site and it fits the environment, it can be there, but it can also be elsewhere.

110639.explanation: Trees are very important. They help against heat, improve air quality, and create a pleasant environment to stay in.

110640.explanation: Yes, but if burning causes nuisance and people do not follow advice, such as not using dry wood or not burning in fog, then monitoring and enforcement is necessary.

110642.explanation: If the municipality itself offers a fun and good alternative, we prevent people from going to Belgium to buy illegal and dangerous fireworks. Besides a municipal fireworks show, each village should also have the opportunity to organize a controlled fireworks show.

110645.explanation: PRO5 believes that everyone should be able to participate and that starts with access to everywhere!

110646.explanation: PRO5 wants circular agriculture, which requires a lot of farmland. Therefore, we are not automatically in favor of housing in rural areas. If a housing project fits well in the area and results in fewer animals in intensive livestock farming, we will support it.

110650.explanation: A village is more than just a collection of houses. Our monuments give the municipality character and make it pleasant to live here.

110651.explanation: The library is a basic facility where not only books are borrowed, but also lectures and training are provided. We therefore find it important that this facility remains easily accessible to everyone.

110652.explanation: There are many agreements and rules in our municipality, and it is important that everyone adheres to them. It helps, also to prevent problems, if enforcement officers are visible in the villages. That gives people a safer feeling and strengthens the sense of community.

110653.explanation: If people feel unsafe, temporary camera surveillance can help improve the situation.

110657.explanation: Given the current pressure on the housing market and the enormous waiting time for social rental housing, many residents no longer have a reasonable alternative.

110659.explanation: If physically and mentally possible, residents participate as much as possible. This is also good for their own well-being.

110660.explanation: Combating poverty is very important to us. This way we prevent residents from getting into further trouble. Forgiving municipal taxes can help prevent larger debts and thus more expensive debt counseling.

110663.explanation: Several charging points have now been created. We would prefer the municipality to invest further in bicycle parking facilities.

110664.explanation: With 30 kilometers per hour, we increase traffic safety in our villages!

110665.explanation: It is legally required that people choose their own healthcare provider. We do find it important that the affordability of care is also considered.

110667.explanation: An increase in property tax (OZB) is already included in the budget for the coming years. Further increases are not desirable.

110669.explanation: An increase in tourist tax is already included in the budget for the coming years. Further increases are not desirable.

110675.explanation: PRO5 places great value on culture and history. Attention to dialects can be part of that and can be encouraged, but it is not necessarily a task for the municipality.

110676.explanation: Today's joint is no longer the “soft drug” of the 1970s. Cannabis addiction leads to serious problems and mental complaints. Therefore, it should not become easier to obtain.

110677.explanation: There is a severe shortage of social housing in our municipality as well. This makes it difficult for young people to continue living in their own village.

110678.explanation: We prefer more duplex homes, such as those currently built on the site of the old sports hall ('t Spant Er) in Bladel. This better suits the rural character of our villages and at the same time provides more homes per square meter. Buildings with three or four residential floors are negotiable.

110679.explanation: Room rental does not yet cause problems in our municipality and can be a solution to the housing shortage. There is currently no reason to ban room rental.

110682.explanation: The Sniederslaan (from the Market to Burgemeester van Houdtplein) will become a safe and car-free shopping area. Accessibility for people with disabilities will also be ensured.

110683.explanation: For entrepreneurs, scaling up or expansion must be subordinate to innovation and further development. Therefore, we currently see no need for a second Kempisch Business Park (KBP) in our municipality.

110684.explanation: The design of the new N284 includes a good solution for the intersection near 'van Mossel'. Two T-junctions will be created to ensure smooth traffic flow.

110686.explanation: Building a caregiver’s home generally does not require a permit. The municipality can be more proactive in informing people about this.

110631.explanation: For important matters, it is wise to engage in dialogue with involved citizens. A survey among residents is possible, but a referendum does not fit within our democratic landscape. Precisely then, an elected body, such as the municipal council, must make a considered decision.

110650.explanation: Monuments must be maintained. They are part of our history. Maintenance costs money and should not be cut. However, one can consider how often a monument requires maintenance. For us, quality prevails over quantity.

110657.explanation: These homes were established under certain conditions, and residents do not fully contribute to social obligations. To comply with these, the designation must be changed. After payment of fees, holiday homes may be used as permanent residences.

110665.explanation: There is sufficient input and choice in the provision of care. It is a socially scarce good to which enormous demands or costs cannot be imposed for the benefit of an individual.

110675.explanation: The Bloals dialect is part of our culture. If a majority wishes to highlight the dialect in primary schools during citizenship or language awareness lessons, this can be facilitated.

110678.explanation: If it fits within the environment, we allow tall residential towers. However, there are restrictions on how high these residential towers may be.

110631.explanation: Establish a citizens’ forum or some other platform where consultation with citizens can take place on important issues.

110634.explanation: The word “always” is not appropriate in this context.

110637.explanation: Every municipality must do its part; that way we solve problems elsewhere.

110638.explanation: We cannot do without labor migrants. A mix of housing near the employer and more large-scale housing is the most suitable. Prevent direct employer ties from leading to exploitation.

110639.explanation: At least one. It is important to initiate new nature development.

110640.explanation: If this means limiting others' enjoyment of living, then we should not allow it.

110642.explanation: If this satisfies a need, yes, but it must also lead to a reduction in illegal fireworks nuisance. It's one or the other.

110645.explanation: It is the entrepreneur’s responsibility. The municipality can serve as an advisor.

110646.explanation: This encourages arbitrariness and does not benefit a structured approach.

110650.explanation: The available financial resources are already very limited.

110651.explanation: According to D66, schools have a role here.

110652.explanation: A “feeling” of safety is a great good. Safety must be experienced close to home.

110653.explanation: Especially in places where safety is at risk, this may be necessary. So it depends on the local situation.

110657.explanation: This is putting the cart before the horse. Temporary solutions may work, but in the long term the housing issue must be addressed. So not permanent, because that plays into the hands of “slumlords.”

110659.explanation: If this means offering people a perspective on a job, yes, but if it is only seen as a counter-performance for benefits, no!

110660.explanation: A drop in the ocean, and what do we solve with it?

110664.explanation: It can only be clear that this applies to all roads within built-up areas.

110665.explanation: This requires no further explanation, as freedom of belief and religion is a great good in the Netherlands.

110667.explanation: If there is still enough space, this means we get cut in municipal fund income and are 'doubly punished.' Ultimately, the residents will be presented with the bill.

110669.explanation: Let those who enjoy our unique environment also contribute to the facilities that this requires.

110675.explanation: D66 does not interfere in schools’ curricula.

110676.explanation: Regulating usage is needed here and provides an opportunity to tackle illegal trade.

110677.explanation: The diversity of home seekers justifies this. It offers opportunities both for starters and for residents who decide to rent at a later age.

110678.explanation: If we want to meet the demand, we cannot avoid it. The question remains: how high do we allow it to go?

110679.explanation: Determining life within a family context can help with this. It prevents landlords from extracting maximum profit from a property. It also creates opportunities for more homes to be available on the market by eliminating unwanted practices.

110682.explanation: This also creates the possibility to strengthen the shopping area in Bladel and make it more of the commercial heart of De Kempen.

110683.explanation: Let's connect the industrial areas of Reusel and Bladel.

110684.explanation: The N284 must be rerouted at the Lange Trekken towards the KBP. This will on one hand provide access for the expansion of Hapert and on the other relieve pressure on the route through Hapert.

110686.explanation: Allow it, but only with a permit, as unchecked growth must be prevented.

statements.title: The provincial road (N284) should be tunneled at Hapert/Van Mossel.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The residents of Bladel should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Bladel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Bladel.

titleParts.text: In Bladel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Bladel should work towards the construction of a second Kempisch Business Park.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: There should be one larger library in Bladel instead of smaller libraries in each village.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety should be employed in Bladel.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be more camera surveillance in Bladel.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Bladel should be social housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Bladel moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Bloals dialect.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Bladel.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The shopping area of Bladel should be made car-free.
The municipality of Bladel should allow the construction of a caregiver's dwelling without a permit.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Bladel moet het bouwen van een

titleParts.glossary: A self-contained dwelling on the grounds of another house, where, for example, grandparents can be cared for.
Permission from the municipality to do something.
